sources.exact_document_title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Gastroenteritis in Children – 07
sources.clinical_setting: Telehealth services in DHA-licensed health facilities.
sources.applicability_note: Direct for pediatric acute-gastroenteritis telehealth documentation in Dubai; partial for any broader vomiting or diarrhea presentation.

exact_sections.heading: 2. Scope; 3. Purpose; 4. Applicability
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines pediatric gastroenteritis use in DHA-licensed telehealth and excludes emergency and controlled-medication scenarios.

exact_sections.heading: 6. Evaluation
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Prioritises red flags, dehydration, severe alternate illness, age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, neurological abnormality, and instability.

exact_sections.heading: 7. Diagnosis
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Defines the acute gastroenteritis symptom pattern and limits laboratory studies to atypical or targeted presentations.

exact_sections.heading: 12. Referral Criteria; 13. Response to Therapy
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists age, fever, bleeding, persistent vomiting, dehydration, neurological, instability, caregiver, and worsening criteria for face-to-face assessment.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 1 — assessment of dehydration
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Lists activity, consciousness, thirst, urine output, mucous membranes, pulse, capillary refill, skin turgor, heart rate, and respiratory signs.

exact_sections.heading: Appendix 2 — important aspects of history
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Specifies duration, stool and emesis character, urine output, intake, fever, abdominal pain, medication, contact, and exposure history.
